./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:45:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_23","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"pod _filter-trusted-issues --label agent-plan --state open --limit 20 --json number,title,body --jq '.[] | \\\"### #\\\\(.number) \\\\(.title)\\\\n\\\\(.body)\\\\n\\\"'\"","aggregated_output":"### #6930 Summarize fifty-fourth Phase 4/5/6 checkpoint\n## Current state\n\nThe last closed summarize issue is #6863, closed at 2026-06-13T15:43:17Z.\nSince then, 21 PRs have merged:\n\n- #6875 `HexPolyFp: add SquareFree constant tail support`\n- #6876 `feat: Phase 6 docstring + annotation polish for HexMatrix/Determinant.lean`\n- #6879 `feat: Phase 6 docstring + annotation polish for HexPoly/Euclid.lean`\n- #6884 `feat: Phase 6 docstring + annotation polish for HexPolyFp/Quotient.lean`\n- #6885 `feat: add Sylvester common-factor column-reduction step`\n- #6891 `feat: add recovered lift representation carrier`\n- #6892 `feat: Phase 6 docstring + annotation polish for HexGF2 Field.lean and Irreducibility.lean`\n- #6899 `feat: assemble common-factor Sylvester column reduction`\n- #6900 `doc: note heartbeat-timeout debugging for heavy Mathlib-layer proofs`\n- #6905 `review: PR 6892 HexGF2 docstring polish`\n- #6908 `HexPolyFp: prove derivative-split quotient cannot be non-one scalar`\n- #6909 `feat: complete HexLLL Phase 5`\n- #6910 `feat: complete HexPolyZ Phase 5 and bump done_through to 5`\n- #6915 `review PR #6908 HexPolyFp derivative-split quotient base`\n- #6916 `doc: record review of PR #6899 (issue #6911)`\n- #6917 `Resultant: explicit-witness common-factor divisibility`\n- #6919 `chore: record review 6913 progress (HexLLL Phase 5)`\n- #6920 `BZ recovery: decouple stale scaled recovery lemmas from representation predicate`\n- #6921 `doc: record review of #6917 (resultant divisibility)`\n- #6922 `doc: record review of PR #6910 (HexPolyZ Phase 5 bump)`\n- #6924 `review PR #6920 BZ recovery scaled-recovery decoupling`\n\nThere is no open summarize issue. The active implementation frontier remains\nthe HO-1 directive #2564 support chain, especially BZ recovered-representation\nwork (#6890 and dependents), HexPolyFp normalized Yun reachability (#6904 and\ndependents), and the resultant common-factor chain (#6849 / PR #6927).\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Write the next concise checkpoint summary covering the merged PRs above,\n   grouped by topic rather than copied one-by-one.\n2. Call out the current frontier and blocking structure for directive #2564,\n   including which merged PRs changed the recovery/resultant/Yun substrate.\n3. Record any phase-state changes visible from `scripts/status.py`, especially\n   HexLLL and HexPolyZ reaching Phase 5 completion, without editing source code.\n\n## Context\n\nUse the previous summarize issue #6863 as the style baseline. Prove a private recursive-step lemma showing that the next gcd cannot be a\n   non-one scalar in a derivative-active reachable step. Suggested shape:\n\n   ```lean\n   private theorem yunStep_gcd_squareFreeContributionReachable\n       (hp : Hex.Nat.Prime p) (f c w : FpPoly p) (fuel : Nat)\n       (hreachable : yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable hp f c w (fuel + 1))\n       (hcReach : squareFreeContributionReachable c) :\n       squareFreeContributionReachable (DensePoly.gcd c w) := ...\n   ```\n\n   Adjust hypotheses if the proof needs the pairwise-reachable projection or\n   nonzero payload explicitly.\n2. Use #6903 plus this step lemma to prove the full structural theorem:\n\n   ```lean\n   private theorem yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable_cReachable\n       (hp : Hex.Nat.Prime p) (f c w : FpPoly p) (fuel : Nat)\n       (hreachable : yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable hp f c w fuel) :\n       squareFreeContributionReachable c := ...\n   ```\n\n3. Combine with `yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable_nonzero` to add the direct\n   `YunDerivativeActiveLevelStateProvider hp` theorem near the provider cluster.\n4. Preserve executable definitions and do not introduce `sorry`, `axiom`,\n   `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n## Context\n\nThe core scalar trap is that a nonzero constant gcd divides `1` but need not be\nsyntactic `1`. The proof must use derivative-active reachability, not just the\nfact that the gcd is a unit. The prior issue comment on #6887 states the intended\nsemantic fact: reachable `(c,w)` pairs are coprime only at the true terminal\nstate, so the raw `c` chain reaches `[1]`, while the raw residual `w` may still\nbe a non-one scalar.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- No new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME` on added lines.\n\n\n### #6902 HexPolyFp: wire Yun level provider into normalized pow invariant\nlibrary: HexPolyFp\n\ndepends-on: #6904\nParent: #6898.\n\n## Current state\n\n#6901 isolates the structural proof that derivative-active reachable raw Yun\n`c` states satisfy `squareFreeContributionReachable c`, yielding a direct\n`YunDerivativeActiveLevelStateProvider hp`.\n\n`HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean` currently proves\n`yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant` from two provider\narguments:\n\n- `hlevelState : YunDerivativeActiveLevelStateProvider hp`\n- `hnormalizedState : YunDerivativeActiveNormalizedStateProvider hp`\n\nCallers in the derivative-active branch still obtain `hlevelState` via\n`YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider.levelState`, which is stronger than needed\nand routes through the false raw-residual provider.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nAfter #6901 lands, in `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean`:\n\n1. Replace derivative-active uses of `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider.levelState`\n   for `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant` with the direct\n   level provider from #6901.\n2. Keep using `yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable_normalized_stateProvider hp` for\n   the normalized provider argument.\n3. Remove any now-unused local `hrawState`/`hlevelState` plumbing from the\n   normalized-pow path, but do not broaden scope into unrelated raw-tail proofs.\n4. Preserve executable definitions (`yunFactorsContributionWithLevel`,\n   `squareFreeAuxRevContribution`, `squareFreeAuxRev`, `squareFreeAux`).\n5. Do not introduce `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n## Context\n\nThe raw residual `w` can be a non-one scalar, so this issue must not recreate or\nrequire `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider`. The target is only the existing\n`hlevelState` argument needed by the current normalized-pow theorem.\n\nUseful declarations:\n\n- the direct level provider from #6901\n- `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant`\n- `yunFactorsDerivativeActiveReachable_normalized_stateProvider`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- No new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME` on added lines.\n\n\n\n### #6890 BZ recovery: swap lifted representation predicate to monic-coordinate carrier\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\nParent: #6881. Depends on #6886 and #6888.\n\n## Current state\n\n#6881's direct predicate replacement is sound in shape but too broad as one step. Two prerequisites should land first:\n\n- #6886 introduces the corrected monic-coordinate carrier and exact recovery API via `liftedRecoveryCandidate_eq_factor_of_congruence_of_bound`.\n- #6888 removes stale scaled-product recovery lemmas' dependency on `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift`.\n\nOnce those are in place, `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` can become the corrected carrier without forcing impossible old scaled-product equalities.\n\nA direct swap also exposed a second non-local obligation near `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean:8639`: `representsIntegerFactorAtLift_mul_of_monic_core` destructs the old congruence directly. Under the corrected existential predicate, this lemma needs an explicit primitive-part/Gauss argument for multiplying monic-coordinate witnesses, or it should be restated against a more appropriate carrier if the current statement is not load-bearing.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Redefine `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` to the corrected monic-coordinate carrier shape from #6886, or make it a transparent alias to that carrier if that is the cleanest local pattern.\n- Update `henselLiftData_represents_lifted_of_modP` to produce the new predicate in the monic case with witness `factor`, using `ZPoly.dilate_one` and `primitivePart_eq_self_of_primitive` from `zpoly_primitive_of_monic`.\n- Update `HenselSubsetCorrespondenceHypotheses`, `HenselSubsetLiftHypotheses`, `HenselLiftDescentHypotheses`, `LiftedFactorSubsetPartition`, `liftedTrueSupports`, and their direct constructors/projections so their fields build against the corrected predicate.\n- Repair or restate `representsIntegerFactorAtLift_mul_of_monic_core`. Do not add a stale scaled-product field to the new predicate just to keep this theorem compiling.\n- Route exact recovery wrappers for `liftedRecoveryCandidate` through #6886/#6838's monic-coordinate API, with bounds on the witnessed monic factor.\n\n## Context\n\nSearch target:\n\n```bash\nrg -n \"RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift|representsIntegerFactorAtLift_mul_of_monic_core|henselLiftData_represents_lifted_of_modP|liftedTrueSupports\" HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean\n```\n\nConcrete direct-swap failures observed during #6881 decomposition:\n\n- `Basic.lean:2675`: stale scaled-product recovery theorem expected old congruence from `hrep`.\n- `Basic.lean:8639`: monic multiplicative closure tried to rewrite `scaledLiftedFactorProduct` after the target became an existential corrected predicate.\n- Baseline also has the known unrelated #6804 failure at `Basic.lean:16402`.\n\n## state\n\n`yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant` currently returns both\nraw and normalized-tail product conjuncts, but it obtains both by routing through\nraw `yunFactorsLevelCompletes` via a `YunDerivativeActiveLevelStateProvider`.\nThat provider is derivable only from `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider`, which\nis the false scalar-unit obligation #6882 is removing.\n\n#6898 (the corrected re-scope of #6887) is the prerequisite that should provide the normalized-tail product theorem\nwithout the raw completion route.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nAfter #6898 lands, in `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean`:\n\n1. Update `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation` and\n   downstream weighted-product callers so the derivative-active path no longer\n   requires `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider` merely to prove the normalized\n   tail product.\n3. Preserve executable definitions (`yunFactorsContributionWithLevel`,\n   `squareFreeAuxRevContribution`, `squareFreeAuxRev`, `squareFreeAux`).\n4. Do not introduce `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n## Context\n\nThis is the second half of #6882. It should be a wiring/refactor issue once the\nnew product theorem exists.\n\nRelevant declarations:\n\n- `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_correct_pow_of_nonzero`\n- `squareFree_weightedProduct` and callers with `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- No new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME` on added lines.\n\n\n\n\n### #6883 HexPolyFp: remove raw Yun state from square-free weighted product\nParent: #6872. Depends on #6889.\n\n## Current state\n\n#6872 asks to remove the explicit public\n`hrawState : YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider hp` argument from the square-free\nweighted-product correctness chain. The current call chain is:\n\n- `squareFree_weightedProduct`\n- `squareFreeAux_weightedProduct_nonzero`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_correct`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_correct_pow_of_nonzero`\n- `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation`\n\nThe derivative-active obligation currently obtains raw tail reachability through\n`YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider.rawTail` and calls\n`derivative_active_raw_tail_weighted_product_bridge_via_normalized`, which in\nturn invokes the IH on raw `pthRoot contribution.2`. That is the false-provider\npath from #6871/#6872: raw repeated tails can be non-monic scalar constants, so\n`squareFreeContributionReachable (pthRoot tail)` is not a valid public premise.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- After #6889 supplies the normalized-only contribution product invariant (the\n  rewired `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_normalized_pow_invariant`, built on\n  #6898's sound level-reachability proof), add the scalar-aware residual bridge for\n  `squareFreeAuxRevContribution (pthRoot tail)` against\n  `squareFreeAuxRevContribution (pthRoot (normalizeMonic tail).2)` without any\n  raw `squareFreeContributionReachable (pthRoot tail)` premise.\n- Update `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation`,\n  `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_correct_pow_of_nonzero`,\n  `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_correct`, `squareFreeAux_weightedProduct_nonzero`,\n  and public `squareFree_weightedProduct` so no explicit\n  `YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider hp` argument remains and no replacement\n  false public hypothesis is introduced.\n- Remove or demote now-unused raw-provider helpers (`YunDerivativeActiveRawStateProvider.rawTail`,\n  `derivative_active_raw_tail_weighted_product_bridge_via_normalized`, and the\n  `_of_nontrivial` wrapper) if they become dead.\n- Preserve raw executable recursion shapes in `squareFreeAuxRevContribution`,\n  `squareFreeAuxRev`, and `squareFreeDecomposition`.\n\n## Context\n\nThis is the direct remainder of #6872 once the normalized product invariant is no\nlonger routed through raw completion. The existing constant-tail facts from\n#6871 remain relevant: `derivative_isZero_true_of_size_one`,\n`pthRoot_eq_self_of_size_one`, and `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_size_one`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `lake build HexPolyFp`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\ndepends-on: #6889\n\n\n\n### #6868 HO-1 FactorSoundness: expose non-associated headline contract\n## Current state\n\nDirective #2564's headline theorem requires the default public factorization to\nhave no associated duplicate factors, not merely no syntactically duplicate\n`ZPoly` keys. The current public bundle in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`,\n`factor_headline_contract_core`, still exposes:\n\n```lean\nList.Pairwise (fun a b : Hex.ZPoly × Nat => a.1 ≠ b.1)\n  (Hex.factor f).factors.toList\n```\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean` already contains the stronger\nbridge:\n\n```lean\nfactor_entries_not_associated\n```\n\nbut it is conditional on the same selected-raw-branch primitive package that\nthe primitive headline issue closes. Once that package is closed, the final\nFactorSoundness wrapper should consume it and expose the SPEC headline\nnon-association clause.\n\nThis issue supports directive #2564 and is intentionally a packaging issue:\ndo not re-prove branch irreducibility or raw primitive facts here.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Add a public `FactorSoundness.lean` theorem bundling the HO-1 headline\n   clauses already available after #6672 and the raw-primitive closure issue:\n   product preservation, primitive plus Mathlib irreducibility per entry,\n   positive multiplicities, pairwise non-association after transport to\n   `Polynomial ℤ`, and signed-content scalar.\n2. Replace the syntactic distinct-key clause in the new wrapper with\n   `List.Pairwise (fun a b => ¬ Associated (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial a.1)\n   (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial b.1))`.\n3. Keep `factor_headline_contract_core` and the primitive intermediate wrapper\n   available; this issue should add the stronger public headline surface rather\n   than breaking existing consumers.\n\n## Context\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`:\n  `factor_headline_contract_core`, `factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`:\n  `factor_entries_not_associated`,\n  `zpoly_not_associated_of_ne_of_primitive_pos_leading`.\n- #6672 closes `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit`, which the headline wrapper\n  needs for the Mathlib irreducibility clause.\n- This issue depends on the raw primitive closure issue created in the same\n  planning cycle.\n\ndepends-on: #6672\ndepends-on: #6867\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.FactorSoundness`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no new\n  `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n\n### #6867 HO-1 FactorSoundness: close raw primitive package for public factor\n## Current state\n\nDirective #2564 requires the public HO-1 headline theorem to state that every\npolynomial factor emitted by `Hex.factor` is primitive and irreducible.\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean` already has\n`factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`, but it is still conditional on\nan explicit raw-branch hypothesis:\n\n```lean\nh_raw :\n  forall rawFactors,\n    (fast rawFactors or slow-modular rawFactors or slow-trial rawFactors) ->\n      forall raw in rawFactors, Hex.ZPoly.Primitive raw\n```\n\nThe executable layer already exposes the dispatch-source shape through\n`Hex.factorWithBound_eq_factorizationOfFactors`,\n`Hex.factor_entry_primitive_of_chosen_raw_primitive`, and related helpers in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`. H).projectedVectorArray_sq_le_of_sum_le\n      v _ (Nat.cast_nonneg _) hnorm_le i\n\n/--\nCanonical weighted-correction pointwise bound for the cap-lift bad-vector\nbridge.\n\nChoosing f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 →\n        (((bridge.auxiliaryCorrections v hin hnot).getD j 0 : ℝ)) ^ 2 ≤ D) :\n    ∀ j, j < (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 →\n (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n      (bridge.auxiliaryCorrections v hin hnot) D hp hD j hj\n```\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n\n### #6819 feat: migrate lifted partition consumers to recovered representation\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\nParent: #6772. Depends on #6890. Consumers include #6774, #6786, #6792, and the blocked #6804 recovery-chain remodel.\n\n## Current state\n\nThis issue is the consumer-migration follow-up after the lifted representation predicate is swapped to the corrected recovered/monic-coordinate shape.\n\nThe old predecessor wording is stale:\n\n- #6838 has landed the precision-gated `liftedRecoveryCandidate` equality and helper facts.\n- #6839 was closed as unsound because its requested dilated-product congruence cannot route through #6838.\n- #6881 corrected the predicate shape to the monic-factor carrier, then decomposed into #6886, #6888, and #6890.\n- #6890 is the live prerequisite that actually swaps `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` and the direct package fields to the corrected carrier. This issue should stay blocked until #6890 lands.\n\nAfter #6890, the downstream API packages still need to be checked and repaired against the new meaning:\n\n- `HenselSubsetCorrespondenceHypotheses`\n- `LiftedFactorSubsetPartition`\n- `InitialLiftedFactorSubsetPartitionEvidence`\n- slow-path substrate constructors such as `liftedFactorSubsetPartition_of_choosePrimeData`, `slowPathHenselSubstrate_of_choosePrimeData`, `slowPathHenselSubstrate_of_toMonicChoosePrimeData`, and their `_success_descent` siblings\n- `liftedTrueSupports` and the bridge from #6786\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Keep the downstream public package fields building against the corrected `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` shape from #6890. Do not weaken the fields to avoid the recovered-candidate obligation.\n- Update any support-subset/divisibility lemmas that mention `scaledRecombinationCandidate core d T` so they use the corrected recovered-candidate/monic-coordinate API rather than the old scalar candidate.\n- Ensure the substrate constructors still thread the corrected representation through the initial full-universe partition and success-descent wrappers.\n- Keep `liftedTrueSupports` semantically unchanged: it should enumerate nonempty represented subsets for true irreducible core factors, but using the corrected representation predicate.\n\n## Context\n\nSearch target:\n\n```bash\nrg -n \"RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift|scaledRecombinationCandidate|LiftedFactorSubsetPartition|InitialLiftedFactorSubsetPartitionEvidence|liftedTrueSupports|slowPathHenselSubstrate\" HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean\n```\n\nThis remains intentionally late in the decomposition because it is mostly consumer repair once the definition/predicate semantics are stable.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- No new `sorry` / `axiom` / `native_decide` / `TODO` / `FIXME` on added lines.\ndepends-on: #6890\n\n\n### #6805 feat: recombine non-monic cores in the fast BHKS path via ZPoly.dilate\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhaus / HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\ndepends-on: #6804\n\n## Context\n\nOptional fourth deliverable of #6801. After #6802 the slow exhaustive path\nrecombines non-monic cores correctly via `ZPoly.dilate`, but the fast BHKS\nper-class candidate `bhksIndicatorCandidate?`\n(`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:5297`) is still scalar-scale based. It\nguards every candidate with `exactQuotient?` and returns `none` on failure\n(no `#[core]` fallback), so for non-monic cores it is **incomplete, not\nunsound**: it cleanly defers them to the slow path. This issue makes the fast\npath also recombine non-monic cores.\n\n## Current state\n\n- `bhksIndicatorCandidate?` (`Basic.lean:5297`) uses `scale (leadingCoeff f)`, `reduceModPow`, `centeredLiftPoly`, `normalizeCandidateFactor`, `normalizeFactorSign`, `exactQuotient?`.\n- The slow path `scaledRecombinationSearchModAux` (`:6526`) already uses the `ZPoly.dilate coreLc (centeredLiftPoly product modulus)` shape.\n- The Mathlib recovery model is dilation-based once #6804 lands.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Migrate `bhksIndicatorCandidate?` to build its candidate via `ZPoly.dilate (leadingCoeff f) (centeredLiftPoly product modulus)`, matching the slow path, so the fast path recombines non-monic cores instead of returning `none`.\n2. Update any Mathlib-side model of the BHKS per-class candidate and its mirror proofs to the dilation shape.\n3. Add a regression fixture: a non-monic core that the fast path now factors (rather than deferring), analogous to the #6802 slow-path fixtures.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` green.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib` stays green.\n- Fast path returns a proper factorisation (not `none`) on the new non-monic fixture.\n- No `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME` introduced.\n\n\n### #6804 feat: migrate the scaledRecombinationCandidate recovery and coverage chain to the dilation model\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\ndepends-on: #6818\ndepends-on: #6819\n\n## Context\n\nThis is the residual of #6801 after the foundation (#6803, the\n`ZPoly.dilate` Mathlib correspondence, now landed) and the predicate\nredesign (originally #6772). #6772's Mathlib-layer deliverable was never\nlanded — it was auto-closed by the executable-only PR #6802, then reopened\nand decomposed into #6817 (recovered lifted-factor candidate surface, has\nPR), #6818 (redefine `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift`/`scaledLiftedFactorProduct`\naround the recovered candidate), and #6819 (migrate the lifted-partition\nconsumers). This issue depends on that chain completing (#6818, #6819);\nthe dilation recovery lemmas below are unprovable against the current\nscale-based predicate (their hypotheses are vacuous for non-monic factors).\n\nPost #6802 the executable slow-path candidate is\n`primitivePart (ZPoly.dilate coreLc (centeredLift product))`\n(`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6550`). The Mathlib model candidate\n`scaledRecombinationCandidate` (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean:2249`)\nis still the old `primitivePart (centeredLift (scale coreLc product))`. The\ntwo are different polynomials for non-monic `core`, so the executable seam\nbreaks: `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean:15990` passes a scale-shaped\n`hprefix`/`hcandidate_def` to the now-dilation executable lemma\n`Hex.scaledRecombinationSearchModAux_eq_some_of_step_of_prefix_none`.\n\nThis is currently the **only** error in the file (the rest of the model is\nself-consistent with the old scale shape), but it cannot be patched at the\nseam: the executable emits a genuinely different value, so the model candidate\nmust be redefined to the dilation shape, which cascades through every lemma\nthat unfolds and rewrites the candidate.\n\n## Current state\n\n- `scaledRecombinationCandidate` (`Basic.lean:2249`) and `liftedFactorProductCandidate` (`:2239`) still build the scale/centeredLift shape.\n- The recovery lemmas encode the scale shape and currently compile self-consistently:\n  - `centeredLiftPoly_scaledLiftedFactorProduct_eq_factor_of_recovery*` (`:4887`, `:4911`)\n  - `scaledRecombinationCandidate_eq_factor_of_recovery*` (`:5122`, `:5166`, `:5187`, `:5220`)\n  - `shouldRecord_scaledRecombinationCandidate_of_eq_factor` (`:10048`), `exactQuotient?_scaledRecombinationCandidate_eq_some_of_eq_factor*` (`:10065`, `:10099`)\n  - `natDegree_toPolynomial_scaledRecombinationCandidate_eq_sum*` (`:9703`, `:9816`), `toPolynomial_scaledRecombinationCandidate_squarefree` (`:10764`)\n  - `liftedFactorSubsetPartition_prefix_none_*_scaled_*` (`:14647`, `:14803`) — these build `hprefix`.\n- Coverage induction at `Basic.lean:15788+`; the seam at `:15963` (`hcandidate_def`) and `:15990` (application).\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Redefine `scaledRecombinationCandidate` (and `liftedFactorProductCandidate` if needed) to the executable dilation shape `normalizeFactorSign (primitivePart (dilate (leadingCoeff core) (centeredLiftPoly (liftedFactorProduct d S) (p^k))))`.\n2. Re-prove the recovery lemmas (`scaledRecombinationCandidate_eq_factor_of_recovery*`, `centeredLiftPoly_scaledLiftedFactorProduct_eq_factor_*`, `shouldRecord_*`, `exactQuotient?_*`) using #6803's correspondence (`primitivePart (dilate coreLc g)` recovers `factor`) and #6818's redesigned predicate.\n3. Mechanically update the product/degree-direction lemmas (`natDegree_*`, `*_squarefree`, the `let`-expression text in the prefix-none `_scaled_` lemmas) to mirror the new candidate.\n4. Fix the coverage proof seam at `Basic.lean:15963`/`:15990` so `hcandidate_def`/`hprefix` carry the dilation shape the executable lemma now expects.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib` green.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` stays green (no executable changes).\n- No `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME` introduced (`factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` remains the one pre-existing headline `sorry`).\n\n\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:61:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_32","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' SPEC/Libraries/hex-arith.md\"","aggregated_output":"# hex-arith (no dependencies)\n\nCore integer arithmetic that everything else builds on.\n\n**Contents:**\n\n- Extended GCD for `Nat`, `Int`, and `UInt64`\n- Barrett and Montgomery reduction for `UInt64` modular arithmetic\n  (see below)\n- Modular exponentiation by squaring (using Montgomery internally)\n- GMP FFI extra: `@[extern]` wrapper for `mpz_gcdext` — for big-integer\n  extended GCD / Bezout computations\n- Pure Lean implementations of the same for the proof target\n\n**Barrett reduction** — fast single modular multiplication on `UInt64`\nfor small moduli (`p < 2^32`). Precompute an approximate reciprocal\nof the modulus, then reduce via multiply + shift instead of division.\n\nThe restriction `p < 2^32` is essential: it ensures `a * b < p² < 2^64`\nfits in a single `UInt64`, so the approximation `q = mulHi(T, pinv)`\nworks with a 64-bit reciprocal. For `p ≥ 2^32`, use Montgomery.\n\n```lean\nstructure BarrettCtx (p : UInt64) where\n  p_gt : p.toNat > 1\n  p_lt : p.toNat < 2^32\n  pinv : UInt64\n  pinv_eq : pinv = .ofNat (2 ^ 64 / p.toNat)\n\ndef BarrettCtx.mk (p : UInt64) (hp : p.toNat > 1) (hlt : p.toNat < 2^32) :\n    BarrettCtx p\ndef BarrettCtx.mulMod (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (a b : UInt64) : UInt64\n```\n\nKey properties:\n```lean\ntheorem BarrettCtx.mulMod_lt (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (a b : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) (hb : b < p) :\n    ctx.mulMod a b < p\n\ntheorem BarrettCtx.toNat_mulMod (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (a b : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) (hb : b < p) :\n    (ctx.mulMod a b).toNat = (a.toNat * b.toNat) % p.toNat\n\ntheorem BarrettCtx.mulMod_eq (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (a b : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) (hb : b < p) :\n    ctx.mulMod a b = .mk ⟨(a.toNat * b.toNat) % p.toNat, by omega⟩\n```\n\n**Barrett proof organization.** Five layers (three Barrett-specific,\nsharing Layers 1–2 with Montgomery). Simpler than Montgomery because\nthere's no domain conversion or inverse computation, and the product\nfits in a single `UInt64`.\n\n*Layer 1 — `HexArith/UInt64/Wide.lean` (shared with Montgomery).*\n128-bit arithmetic: `mulHi`, `addCarry`, `subBorrow`. See Montgomery\nsection below.\n\n*Layer 2 — `HexArith/Nat/ModArith.lean` (shared with Montgomery).*\nNat-level modular lemmas. See Montgomery section below.\n\n*Layer 3 — `HexArith/Barrett/ReduceNat.lean` (pure Nat math).* Barrett\nreduction stated and proved at `Nat` with `R = 2^64`. No `UInt64`:\n\n```lean\n/-- Barrett reduction at the Nat level.\n    Given T = a * b (fits in a single word since p < 2^32),\n    pinv = ⌊R / p⌋:\n    1. q = ⌊T * pinv / R⌋   — approximate quotient T / p\n    2. r = T - q * p          — approximate remainder\n    3. if r ≥ p then r - p    — at most one corrective subtraction -/\ndef barrettReduceNat (p pinv T : Nat) : Nat :=\n  let q := T * pinv / R\n  let r := T - q * p\n  if r ≥ p then r - p else r\n```\n\nThe key bound: `pinv = ⌊R / p⌋`, so `R/p - 1 < pinv ≤ R/p`.\n\n```lean\ntheorem barrettReduceNat_eq_mod (hp : 1 < p) (hpinv : pinv = R / p)\n    (hT : T < R) :\n    barrettReduceNat p pinv T = T % p\n\ntheorem barrettReduceNat_lt (hp : 1 < p) (hpinv : pinv = R / p)\n    (hT : T < R) :\n    barrettReduceNat p pinv T < p\n```\n\nNote: the caller provides `T < R` which follows from `a, b < p < 2^32`\ngiving `T = a * b < 2^64 = R`.\n\nProof of `barrettReduceNat_eq_mod`:\n\n1. **Quotient bound.** Let `q_exact = T / p` and `q = T * pinv / R`.\n\n   Upper bound (`q ≤ q_exact`): `pinv = ⌊R/p⌋ ≤ R/p`, so\n   `T * pinv ≤ T * R/p`, hence `q = ⌊T * pinv / R⌋ ≤ T/p`.\n\n   Lower bound (`q ≥ q_exact - 1`): `pinv ≥ R/p - 1`, so\n   `T * pinv ≥ T * (R/p - 1) = T*R/p - T`. Hence\n   `T * pinv / R ≥ T/p - T/R`. Since `T < R`, we get `T/R < 1`,\n   so `q ≥ ⌊T/p - T/R⌋ ≥ T/p - 1 = q_exact - 1`.\n\n2. **Remainder bound.** `r = T - q*p`. Since `q ≤ q_exact`,\n   `r ≥ T mod p ≥ 0`. Since `q ≥ q_exact - 1`,\n   `r ≤ T mod p + p < 2p`. And `2p < 2^33 < R`, so `r` fits\n   in a single `UInt64`.\n\n3. **Conditional subtraction.** If `r ≥ p`, then `r - p = T mod p`.\n   If `r < p`, then `r = T mod p` directly.\n\n*Layer 4 — `HexArith/Barrett/Reduce.lean` (UInt64 bridge).* Executable\nBarrett reduction in `UInt64`, proven equivalent to `barrettReduceNat`.\nSince `p < 2^32`, the product `T = a * b` fits in a single `UInt64`\n(no 128-bit splitting needed):\n\n```lean\ndef barrettReduce (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (T : UInt64) : UInt64 :=\n  let q := mulHi T ctx.pinv\n  let r := T - q * p\n  if r ≥ p then r - p else r\n\ntheorem toNat_barrettReduce (ctx : BarrettCtx p) (T : UInt64)\n    (hT : T.toNat < p.toNat * p.toNat) :\n    (barrettReduce ctx T).toNat =\n      barrettReduceNat p.toNat ctx.pinv.toNat T.toNat\n```\n\nThe bridge is straightforward: `mulHi T pinv` = `T.toNat * pinv.toNat / R`\nby `toNat_mulHi`, and `T - q * p` is safe (no underflow since `q ≤ T/p`\nmeans `q * p ≤ T`). No high-word reasoning needed.\n\n*Layer 5 — `HexArith/Barrett/Context.lean` (user-facing API).*\n`BarrettCtx.mulMod` defined as `barrettReduce ctx (a * b)`. The product\n`a * b` doesn't overflow because `a < p < 2^32` and `b < p < 2^32`\ngive `a.toNat * b.toNat < 2^64`. The three user-facing theorems\n(`mulMod_lt`, `toNat_mulMod`, `mulMod_eq`) follow from\n`toNat_barrettReduce` + `barrettReduceNat_eq_mod`.\n\n**Why Barrett is simpler than Montgomery:** no domain conversion\n(no `toMont`/`fromMont`), no Montgomery inverse computation (no\nNewton iteration), the product fits in a single word, and the Nat-level\nproof is a direct approximation argument rather than a modular-inverse\ncongruence.\n\n**Montgomery reduction** — optimized for sustained modular arithmetic\n(sequences of multiplications with the same modulus, e.g. exponentiation\nby squaring, polynomial evaluation over F_p).\n\nValues are stored in Montgomery form: `a` is represented as `a * R mod p`\nwhere `R = 2^64`. Reduction uses the low bits of the product (a mask)\ninstead of division.\n\n```lean\nstructure MontCtx (p : UInt64) where\n  p_odd : p % 2 = 1\n  p' : UInt64\n  p'_eq : (p'.toNat * p.toNat) % 2 ^ 64 = 2 ^ 64 - 1\n  r2 : UInt64\n  r2_eq : r2.toNat = (2 ^ 64 * 2 ^ 64) % p.toNat\n\ndef MontCtx.mk (p : UInt64) (hp : p % 2 = 1) : MontCtx p\ndef MontCtx.toMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a : UInt64) : UInt64\ndef MontCtx.fromMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a : UInt64) : UInt64\ndef MontCtx.mulMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a b : UInt64) : UInt64\n```\n\nKey properties:\n```lean\ntheorem MontCtx.fromMont_toMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) :\n    ctx.fromMont (ctx.toMont a) = a\n\ntheorem MontCtx.toNat_mulMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a b : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) (hb : b < p) :\n    (ctx.fromMont (ctx.mulMont (ctx.toMont a) (ctx.toMont b))).toNat =\n      (a.toNat * b.toNat) % p.toNat\n\ntheorem MontCtx.mulMont_eq (ctx : MontCtx p) (a b : UInt64)\n    (ha : a < p) (hb : b < p) :\n    ctx.fromMont (ctx.mulMont (ctx.toMont a) (ctx.toMont b)) =\n      .mk ⟨(a.toNat * b.toNat) % p.toNat, by omega⟩\n```\n\n**Montgomery proof organization.** The correctness proof is split into\nlayers that separate pure math from machine-word encoding. Lean 4 core\nprovides `UInt64.toNat_add`, `toNat_mul`, `toNat_div`, `toNat_mod`,\n`toNat_sub` (all `simp`-tagged), which suffice for overflow reasoning\nvia `Nat.add_mod`, `Nat.mul_mod`, `Nat.div_add_mod`. There is no\n`mulHi`, add-with-carry, or `UInt128` in core — we build those.\n\n*Layer 1 — `HexArith/UInt64/Wide.lean` + `HexArith/ffi/wide_arith.c`\n(shared with Barrett).* 128-bit arithmetic helpers on `UInt64`. Each\noperation has a logical Nat-level body for proofs **and** a mandatory\n`@[extern]` C extern for runtime; the in-Lean `.ofNat (...)` body\nwould compile to GMP arbitrary-precision multiplication and is at\nleast an order of magnitude slower than the native instruction. Both\nmust land together — adding the Lean definition without the extern\nis a regression, not a scaffold.\n\n```lean\n@[extern \"lean_hex_uint64_mul_hi\"]\ndef UInt64.mulHi (a b : UInt64) : UInt64 :=\n  .ofNat (a.toNat * b.toNat / 2^64)\n\n@[extern \"lean_hex_uint64_mul_full\"]\ndef UInt64.mulFull (a b : UInt64) : UInt64 × UInt64 :=\n  let p := a.toNat * b.toNat\n  (.ofNat (p / 2^64), .ofNat p)  -- (hi, lo)\n\n@[extern \"lean_hex_uint64_add_carry\"]\ndef UInt64.addCarry (a b : UInt64) (cin : Bool) : UInt64 × Bool\n\n@[extern \"lean_hex_uint64_sub_borrow\"]\ndef UInt64.subBorrow (a b : UInt64) (bin : Bool) : UInt64 × Bool\n\ntheorem UInt64.toNat_mulHi (a b : UInt64) :\n    (mulHi a b).toNat = a.toNat * b.toNat / 2^64\ntheorem UInt64.mulHi_mulLo (a b : UInt64) :\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:62:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' PLAN/Phase6.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Phase 6: Proof Polishing\n\n**Coupling:** local. Library L can start Phase 6 once\n`libraries.yml[L].done_through ≥ 5`.\n\nBring sorry-free proofs to Mathlib quality. This is substantially more\nthan mechanical cleanup.\n\n## Granularity\n\nEvery non-trivial definition or theorem needs separate treatment here.\nThis is not a \"run a linter and clean up\" pass — it requires careful\nthought about API design for each declaration.\n\n## What Mathlib quality means\n\n- **Good API design.** Right level of generality; clean theorem\n  statements that are easy to apply downstream.\n- **Encapsulation via characterising lemmas.** Don't force users to\n  unfold definitions; provide the lemmas that characterise each\n  definition's behavior.\n- **Avoiding defeq abuse.** Don't rely on definitional equality for\n  things that should be stated as lemmas.\n- **Appropriate automation annotations.** `@[simp]` lemmas for\n  normalization, `@[grind]` for automated reasoning. Maximize what\n  `simp` and `grind` can close automatically.\n- **Clean imports and namespace management.** Minimal imports, proper\n  `open` scoping, no namespace pollution.\n- **Follow Mathlib's published local standards.** In particular:\n  - contribution guide:\n    <https://leanprover-community.github.io/contribute/index.html>\n  - style guide:\n    <https://leanprover-community.github.io/contribute/style.html>\n  - naming conventions:\n    <https://leanprover-community.github.io/contribute/naming.html>\n  - documentation style:\n    <https://leanprover-community.github.io/contribute/doc.html>\n\nThis project is not committing to upstreaming as part of the plan; the\npoint of these references is to set a local quality bar for bridge\nlibraries and proof-heavy cleanup.\n\n## Exit criteria\n\nFor library `hex-foo`, Phase 6 is done when:\n\n- the Mathlib linter is clean on the library;\n- docstring coverage meets the rule in `SPEC/design-principles.md`\n  (public declarations and non-obvious private helpers);\n- the library contains no dead or unreferenced declarations;\n- the library passes a performance regression check against the\n  previous tagged benchmark baseline if one exists, otherwise\n  against the most recent committed benchmark baseline (the\n  bootstrap case before any release has been tagged);\n- **the library's computational path runs natively in Lean** (no\n  `native_decide`, no reliance on external oracles at runtime);\n- **any irreducibility or field-construction claim exposed by the\n  library is backed by Lean-checked evidence**, not by an external\n  oracle (for libraries that expose such claims — `hex-berlekamp`,\n  `hex-gfq-field`, `hex-conway`, `hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus`, etc.).\n\nThe last two criteria were previously stated as project-wide release\ngates in `PLAN.md`'s \"Release criteria\" section. They belong here as\nper-library Phase 6 exit criteria: if every library in a release set\npasses them, the release inherits the guarantee automatically. See\n[Releases.md](Releases.md) for the genuinely release-level check.\n\nRecord completion by bumping `libraries.yml[L].done_through` to `6`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:83:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_43","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,140p' reports/hex-arith-wide-word-api-phase6-review.md\"","aggregated_output":"# HexArith UInt64 Wide-Word API Phase 6 Review\n\n## Scope\n\nReviewed the shared wide-word substrate in:\n\n- `HexArith/UInt64/Wide.lean`\n- `HexArith/ffi/wide_arith.c`\n- `HexArith/Barrett/Reduce.lean`\n- `HexArith/Montgomery/Redc.lean`\n- `HexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean`\n- `HexArith/Conformance.lean`\n\nThe criteria are `PLAN/Phase6.md` and the Layer 1 requirements in\n`SPEC/Libraries/hex-arith.md`: callers should reason through stable Nat-view\nand reconstruction lemmas, each helper should have both a Lean logical body and\na C extern, and callers needing both halves of the same product should use\n`UInt64.mulFull` rather than separate low/high computations.\n\nThis is a review-only Phase 6 slice. It does not edit Lean or C source.\n\n## Overall Assessment\n\n`HexArith/UInt64/Wide.lean` is close to Phase 6 quality. The public operations\nhave clear logical bodies and matching externs:\n\n- `UInt64.mulHi`\n- `UInt64.mulFull`\n- `UInt64.addCarry`\n- `UInt64.subBorrow`\n\nThe theorem surface is also broadly well-shaped. `toNat_mulHi`,\n`toNat_mulFull`, `toNat_mulFull_fst`, `toNat_mulFull_snd`,\n`mulFull_eq_mulHi_mul`, `mulFull_snd_add_fst`, `toNat_addCarry`, and\n`toNat_subBorrow` let downstream proofs move to exact Nat arithmetic without\nunfolding executable bodies. Component lemmas for carry and borrow bits are\ntagged for automation, and `HexArith/Conformance.lean` covers the intended\noverflow and underflow edge cases.\n\nThe C extern contract is present and direct: multiplication uses\n`__uint128_t`, `mulFull` computes both halves from one wide product, and\ncarry/borrow use compiler overflow builtins. The extern names match the Lean\ndeclarations, and the source is part of the existing `hexarithffi` extern\nlibrary.\n\n## Findings\n\n### 1. Montgomery context still computes two-word products through split calls\n\n`HexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean` defines:\n\n```lean\ndef toMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a : UInt64) : UInt64 :=\n  redc ctx (UInt64.mulHi a ctx.r2) (a * ctx.r2)\n\ndef mulMont (ctx : MontCtx p) (a b : UInt64) : UInt64 :=\n  redc ctx (UInt64.mulHi a b) (a * b)\n```\n\nThis is semantically correct, but it violates the SPEC guidance for callers\nthat need both halves of the same product. These definitions perform a wrapped\nlow multiplication plus a separate `mulHi` extern call instead of destructuring\n`UInt64.mulFull`.\n\nFiled as #6167:\n\n`HexArith Phase 6: route Montgomery context two-word products through mulFull`\n\nSuggested deliverable: refactor `toMont` and `mulMont` to destructure\n`UInt64.mulFull`, and update the local bounds/projection proofs to use\n`toNat_mulFull_fst`, `toNat_mulFull_snd`, or `mulFull_snd_add_fst`.\n\n### 2. REDC proof lemmas expose a split-call view after the executable uses `mulFull`\n\n`HexArith/Montgomery/Redc.lean` correctly implements `redc` with:\n\n```lean\nlet (mhi, mlo) := UInt64.mulFull m p\n```\n\nHowever, `redc_u_spec` states the carry-chain theorem using\n`UInt64.mulHi m p` and `m * p`, and `redc_sub_spec` immediately rewrites\n`UInt64.mulFull m p = (UInt64.mulHi m p, m * p)`. This is a proof-quality\ngap rather than a runtime bug: downstream proofs are still encouraged to reason\nthrough the old split representation instead of the executable `mulFull`\ncomponents.\n\nFiled as #6169:\n\n`HexArith Phase 6: expose REDC carry-chain lemmas in mulFull form`\n\nSuggested deliverable: add or refactor REDC helper lemmas so the main\ncarry-chain specification is stated over the `mhi`/`mlo` pair obtained from\n`UInt64.mulFull m p`, while retaining any split-call bridge as a compatibility\nlemma if existing proofs need it.\n\n### 3. Reconstruction lemmas are useful but under-annotated for automation\n\nThe file has good reconstruction theorems:\n\n- `mulHi_mulLo`\n- `mulLo_add_mulHi`\n- `mulFull_snd_add_fst`\n- `toNat_addCarry`\n- `toNat_subBorrow`\n\nOnly the component projections and bit characterizations are currently tagged\nfor `simp`/`grind`. Current consumers call the reconstruction lemmas manually,\nespecially in `HexArith/Montgomery/Redc.lean`. That is reasonable in long\nproofs, but Phase 6 should audit whether conservative `@[grind =]`\nannotations on the reconstruction equalities let Barrett/Montgomery proofs\navoid boilerplate without causing search blowups.\n\nFiled as #6168:\n\n`HexArith Phase 6: tune automation for UInt64 wide-word reconstruction lemmas`\n\nSuggested deliverable: test `@[grind =]` on the reconstruction lemmas above,\nkeep only annotations that reduce current proof boilerplate without loops, and\nadd small local checks in the affected files or conformance layer.\n\n## Checked Clusters With No Follow-Up Needed\n\n- Public declarations in `HexArith/UInt64/Wide.lean` have docstrings. The\n  private quotient-bound helper also has a docstring because its role is not\n  obvious from the name.\n- Namespace hygiene is clean: the file lives in namespace `UInt64` and imports\n  only `Std`.\n- `Barrett/Reduce.lean` uses `UInt64.mulHi` appropriately. Barrett reduction\n  needs only the high word of `T * pinv`; it does not need both halves.\n- The C externs match the Lean logical bodies and use a single `__uint128_t`\n  multiply for `mulFull`.\n- Conformance checks cover wide products crossing the `2^64` boundary,\n  carry/no-carry addition, borrow/no-borrow subtraction, and optimized\n  Barrett/Montgomery consumers.\n\n## Phase 6 Readiness\n\nThe wide-word API surface itself is solid, but `HexArith` should not treat this\nslice as fully polished until the Montgomery consumers are aligned with the\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

powMod\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:970:      · simp [hfit, hodd, powModWordOdd_eq a n (UInt64.ofNat p) hodd]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:971:      · simp [hfit_lt, hodd, powModNat_eq a n p hp]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:978:      simp [hfit_lt, powModNat_eq a n p hp]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:982:theorem powMod_modulus_zero (a n : Nat) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:983:    powMod a n 0 = 0 := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:988:theorem powMod_zero_exp (a p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:989:    powMod a 0 p = 1 % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:990:  simpa using powMod_eq a 0 p hp\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:994:theorem powMod_one_exp (a p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:995:    powMod a 1 p = a % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:996:  simpa using powMod_eq a 1 p hp\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1000:theorem by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1010:  | succ n => simp [powMod_eq 0 (n + 1) p hp]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1014:theorem powMod_one_base (n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) powMod_modulus_one (a n : Nat) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1021:    powMod a n 1 = 0 := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1022:  rw [powMod_eq a n 1 (by decide)]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1025:/-- Successor-exponent expansion for `powMod`: multiply by the base on the left\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1027:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1028:theorem powMod_succ (a n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1029:    powMod a (n + 1) p = (a * powMod a n p) % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1030:  rw [powMod_eq a (n + 1) p hp, powMod_eq a n p hp, Nat.pow_succ]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1033:/-- Reducing the base before modular exponentiation does not change `powMod`. -/\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1035:theorem powMod_mod_base (a n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1036:    powMod (a % p) n p = powMod a n p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1037:  rw [powMod_eq (a % p) n p hp, powMod_eq a n p hp]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1041:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1042:theorem powMod_mul_base (a b n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1043:    powMod (a * b) n p = (powMod a n p * powMod b n p) % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1044:  rw [powMod_eq (a * b) n p hp, powMod_eq a n p hp, powMod_eq b n p hp,\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1047:/-- Exponent-addition expansion for `powMod`: combine two exponentiation phases\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1049:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1050:theorem powMod_add_exp (a m n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1051:    powMod a (m + n) p = (powMod a m p * powMod a n p) % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1052:  rw [powMod_eq a (m + n) p hp, powMod_eq a m p hp, powMod_eq a n p hp,\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1055:/-- Left-oriented companion of `powMod_add_exp`. -/\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1056:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1057:theorem powMod_add_exp_left (a m n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1058:    powMod a (m + n) p = (powMod a n p * powMod a m p) % p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1059:  rw [powMod_add_exp a m n p hp, Nat.mul_comm]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1061:/-- Exponent-multiplication composition for `powMod`: an exponent product is a\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1063:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1064:theorem powMod_mul_exp (a m n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1065:    powMod a (m * n) p = powMod (powMod a m p) n p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1066:  rw [powMod_eq a (m * n) p hp, powMod_eq (powMod a m p) n p hp,\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1067:    powMod_eq a m p hp, Nat.pow_mul]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1070:/-- Swap-oriented companion of `powMod_mul_exp`. -/\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1071:@[grind =>]\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1072:theorem powMod_mul_exp_swap (a m n p : Nat) (hp : p > 0) :\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1073:    powMod a (m * n) p = powMod (powMod a n p) m p := by\nHexArith/Montgomery/Context.lean:1074:  rw [Nat.mul_comm m n, powMod_mul_exp a n m p hp]\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:91:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_47","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,180p' PLAN/Phase1.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Phase 1: Scaffolding\n\n**Coupling:** dep-coupled. Library L can start Phase 1 once every\n`d ∈ L.deps` has `libraries.yml[d].done_through ≥ 1`.\n\nCreate Lean files implementing each library's API as declared in the\nSPEC. Parallelizable in DAG order (a library can be scaffolded once\nits dependencies are scaffolded).\n\n## What to scaffold\n\nRead `SPEC/Libraries/hex-foo.md`. The SPEC contains explicit Lean\ncode blocks with `structure`, `def`, `theorem`, and `class`\ndeclarations, and some newer library specs also give explicit API\ncontracts in prose with named suggested declarations. These stable\ndeclarations and record shapes are the scaffolding targets. Prose\ndiscussion of proof strategies, alternatives, heuristics, and\nexamples is context for later phases, not a scaffolding obligation,\nunless it explicitly fixes the public API or theorem split.\n\n## Rules\n\n- **Read the SPEC prose for each declaration before writing its\n  body.** The signature alone is not the contract. Locate the SPEC\n  prose that describes *what algorithm* the declaration computes,\n  read it end to end, and make sure your body implements that\n  algorithm. If the SPEC gives explicit update formulas, case\n  analysis, or a pseudocode sketch, your body must follow it. If\n  you cannot find such prose, or cannot understand it, do not\n  commit the declaration.\n\n- Every `def`, data-carrying `structure` field, `class`, and\n  `instance` MUST have a body that the author *believes*\n  correctly implements the SPEC contract for that declaration.\n  There is no acceptable placeholder form:\n  - NOT wrong-but-plausible bodies\n    (`def rref M := { rank := 0, echelon := M, transform := 1 }`).\n  - NOT `sorry` bodies (`noncomputable def rref ... := sorry`).\n  - NOT `axiom rref : ...`.\n  - NOT trivial returns (`Matrix.identity`, `none`, the input\n    unchanged, an identity cast).\n  - NOT alternative implementations with the wrong algorithmic\n    complexity. **In particular**: a function whose name or\n    signature promises a *native-typed* algorithm (`UInt64`,\n    `UInt32`, `Float`, `Fin n`, ...) but whose body is `f a.toNat\n    b.toNat` followed by `.ofNat` of the result is a wrong-shape\n    scaffold — it moves all the work into bignum `Nat` arithmetic,\n    which is the wrong asymptotic and the wrong algorithm. Native\n    type signatures must perform native arithmetic.\n  - NOT a body marked \"honest placeholder\", \"Phase 1 scaffold\n    returns <trivial>\", \"scaffold for the eventual <X> bridge\",\n    \"for now\", or any equivalent phrase in its docstring or\n    comments. Such phrases are meta-commentary about implementation\n    history, not documentation; their presence is itself the bug.\n  - NOT a fake `@[extern \"name\"]` whose C body delegates straight\n    back to the Lean fallback. An `@[extern]` whose only effect is\n    to call `l_Hex_*___boxed` (or otherwise re-enter the Lean\n    runtime to do the actual work) is not a valid extern boundary.\n    Either the C side does work native to C (calling GMP, CLMUL,\n    `__uint128_t`, etc.), or the `@[extern]` attribute and its C\n    file must not be committed yet.\n\n  If you cannot implement the function correctly in this PR: **do\n  not commit the declaration**. Leave it out of Lean entirely. The\n  SPEC file is the record of what's *designed*; the Lean surface\n  is the record of what's *implemented correctly so far*. Any bad\n  definitions will poison all downstream conformance testing,\n  benchmarking, and theorem proving. We need to get definitions\n  right, and if we ever discover they are wrong, fix them\n  immediately rather than working around them.\n\n- **`sorry`'d theorem proofs are expected.** The point of scaffolding\n  is to get the API surface compiling, not to fill in proofs. This\n  rule applies to `theorem` bodies and propositional `structure`\n  fields — those produce `Prop` values, and a `sorry` proof doesn't\n  propagate into computation. It does **not** extend an escape\n  valve for data-level bodies — see the rule above.\n\n- **Helper definitions** not in the SPEC are fine if needed to state\n  the API. Note them in the PR. The same \"correctly implemented or\n  not committed\" rule applies.\n\n- **Verify:** `lake build` must succeed after each scaffolding PR.\n\n## Work unit granularity (Phase 1)\n\nPhase 1 issues are typically one-per-major-structure or\none-per-SPEC-subsection — whichever matches the shape of the SPEC\nfile. A single structure definition plus its basic API lemmas is\noften the right size. Every library will need many PRs across many\nagent sessions.\n\nGeneral issue-writing conventions (narrow issues, canonical body\nshape, decomposition, partial progress) live in\n[Conventions.md](Conventions.md).\n\n## Exit criteria\n\nFor library `hex-foo`, Phase 1 is done when:\n\n- every SPEC declaration that has been implemented in Lean has a\n  signature matching the SPEC, and a body that correctly implements\n  the SPEC contract (no `sorry`, no `axiom`, no wrong-but-plausible\n  trivial body);\n- SPEC declarations that have *not* yet been correctly implemented\n  are not present in Lean — the PR description records which ones\n  are deferred to follow-ups, and a follow-up issue exists for each;\n- `lake build <HexFooLib>` succeeds;\n- each new `.lean` file carries a module docstring summarising the\n  library contents;\n- no `TODO`, `FIXME`, or `...` placeholder remains in the scaffolded\n  code (other than `sorry` in proofs).\n\nRecord completion by bumping `libraries.yml[L].done_through` to `1`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:93:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_48","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,180p' SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md\"","aggregated_output":"# hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus (the capstone)\n\nDepends on hex-berlekamp + hex-hensel + hex-lll.\n\nComplete factoring of univariate polynomials over `Z`.\n\nThis library exposes a stable public factoring API delivered by a\n**two-tier architecture**: a fast van Hoeij CLD lattice path\n(`factorFast`, returning `Option`, conditionally correct), an\nexhaustive slow path (`factorSlow`, unconditionally correct), and an\nunconditionally-correct combinator `factor` defaulting to fast with\nfallback to slow. Both paths are first-class top-level functions; the\nfast path is the production code path; the slow path is the verified\nbackstop. No `axiom` declarations are introduced in this library or\nits Mathlib bridge; every theorem has a real proof.\n\nThe public API accepts arbitrary input polynomials and normalizes\ninternally: extract content, remove powers of `X`, and reduce to the\nprimitive square-free case — then make that square-free core monic via\nthe integral-normalisation transform `ZPoly.toMonic`\n(`c^(deg−1)·core(X/c)`, `c = leadingCoeff`), so Hensel lifting and\nrecombination see a monic polynomial and factors are scaled back\nafterward — before running the recombination pipeline.\nThe output is a **`Factorization` record** explicitly separating the\nsigned scalar (sign · content) from the polynomial-factor multiset\nwith explicit multiplicities. Factor order in the polynomial-factor\narray is operationally the array order, but the mathematical contract\nis through product and membership rather than any semantic significance\nof that order.\n\n**Top-level API:**\n```lean\ndef factorSlow (f : ZPoly) : Factorization\ndef factorFast (f : ZPoly) : Option Factorization\ndef factorWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Factorization :=\n  (factorFastWithBound f B).getD (factorSlowWithBound f B)\ndef factor (f : ZPoly) : Factorization :=\n  factorWithBound f (ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f)\n```\n\n`factorSlow` is the unconditionally-correct exhaustive recombination\npath; `factorFast` is the proof-facing van Hoeij CLD path exposing the\ncombined BHKS/Mignotte precision cap (`factorFastPrecisionCap`);\n`factorWithBound` is the bounded combinator shared by the slow and\nfast paths at a caller-supplied precision; `factor` is the public\nentry point and uses the runtime-oriented Mignotte coefficient bound\n`ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound` for both paths.\n\nThe public `factor` does **not** call `factorFast` at the full BHKS\nthreshold. Irreducible inputs that split modulo the chosen prime\n(e.g. `X^4 + 1`, `Φ_15`) force the van Hoeij doubling loop to grind\nthrough every precision up to `bhksBound f`, which is intractable\neven on the small conformance corpus. The smaller Mignotte cap keeps\npublic `factor` runtime within the per-call CI budget and falls\nthrough to `factorSlow` whenever the fast attempt does not yield a\nrecombination certificate. `factorFast` itself is retained at the\nfull BHKS cap as the proof-facing entry point: its conditional-\ncorrectness theorem (Group B) and the eventual BHKS Theorem 5.2\ntermination guarantee (Group D, leaf) apply to the BHKS-capped\ncombinator.\n\n**Output convention: the `Factorization` record.**\n\n```lean\nstructure Factorization where\n  /-- Signed scalar absorbing both sign and content of the input.\n      For nonzero input: `scalar = sign(lc(f)) · ZPoly.content(f)`.\n      For `f = 0`: `scalar = 0`. -/\n  scalar  : Int\n  /-- Polynomial factors (each irreducible primitive with positive\n      leading coefficient) with multiplicities ≥ 1; no two pairs\n      share a polynomial. -/\n  factors : Array (ZPoly × Nat)\nderiving DecidableEq\n\ndef Factorization.product (φ : Factorization) : ZPoly :=\n  φ.factors.foldl (fun acc ⟨g, m⟩ => acc * g^m) (DensePoly.C φ.scalar)\n```\n\nFor `f : ZPoly`, `factor f` returns a `Factorization` such that:\n\n1. `scalar = sign(lc(f)) · ZPoly.content(f)`. Zero iff `f = 0`.\n2. Each `(g, m) ∈ factors` has:\n   - `g` primitive with positive leading coefficient\n     (`Hex.ZPoly.Primitive g ∧ 0 < g.leadingCoeff`),\n   - `g` irreducible (`Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible g`; see below),\n   - `m > 0`.\n3. **No duplicate polynomial factors**: distinct entries in `factors`\n   have distinct first components.\n4. **Product preservation**: `Factorization.product (factor f) = f`.\n5. Factor order is operationally array order; the mathematical\n   contract is product + membership (an array of pairs as a multiset).\n\n**Convention: don't break content into primes.** `factor 6 = ⟨6, #[]⟩`,\nnot `⟨1, #[(C 2, 1), (C 3, 1)]⟩`. The `factors` field carries\n*polynomial* factors of `primitivePart(f)`; the integer\nfactorisation of the content lives in the `scalar` field as a single\nsigned integer. This matches FLINT and SymPy.\n\n### Edge cases\n\n| Input `f` | `factor f` |\n|---|---|\n| `0` | `⟨0, #[]⟩` |\n| `1` | `⟨1, #[]⟩` |\n| `-1` | `⟨-1, #[]⟩` |\n| `2` | `⟨2, #[]⟩` |\n| `-6` | `⟨-6, #[]⟩` |\n| `X` | `⟨1, #[(X, 1)]⟩` |\n| `-X` | `⟨-1, #[(X, 1)]⟩` |\n| `X²` | `⟨1, #[(X, 2)]⟩` |\n| `-X² + 1` | `⟨-1, #[(X-1, 1), (X+1, 1)]⟩` |\n| `(X-1)²` | `⟨1, #[(X-1, 2)]⟩` |\n| `-(X-1)²` | `⟨-1, #[(X-1, 2)]⟩` |\n| `2(X-1)(X+1)` | `⟨2, #[(X-1, 1), (X+1, 1)]⟩` |\n| `-2(X-1)²` | `⟨-2, #[(X-1, 2)]⟩` |\n\n**Why this representation, vs. `Array ZPoly` with content folded as\na constant element?** Two reasons:\n\n1. The signed-scalar field gives uniform sign handling. With a flat\n   `Array ZPoly`, sign would have to be encoded as a constant-\n   polynomial factor or as a separately-tracked `Int`, neither\n   ergonomic. The Mathlib-bridge product theorem\n   `Factorization.product (factor f) = f` then becomes provably\n   exact (no \"up to sign\" caveat).\n2. Multiplicity is explicit, matching how mature CAS systems\n   (FLINT's `fmpz_poly_factor_t`, SageMath's `Factorization`,\n   SymPy's `factor_list`, Mathematica's `FactorList`) represent\n   integer-polynomial factorisations.\n\n**Mathlib-free `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible` class.**\n\n```lean\nnamespace Hex.ZPoly\n\n/-- Mathlib-free irreducibility for ZPoly. Defined as a `class` (not\n    a plain `Prop`) so that downstream APIs that require `p`\n    irreducible — particularly `NumberField.Inv` and the `Field`\n    instance on `NumberField p x` in `hex-number-field` — can use it\n    as an instance argument and have `x⁻¹` notation work via\n    typeclass inference.\n\n    A bare `Prop` would force inverse-related operations to take\n    explicit hypothesis arguments, breaking the `Inv` typeclass\n    contract. Mathlib's `Fact` wrapper would solve this at the\n    Mathlib level, but `Fact` is unavailable in our Mathlib-free\n    setting.\n\n    This contradicts the project's usual \"irreducibility is a\n    term-level fact, not a class\" convention (cf.\n    `HexGFqField.FiniteField`'s explicit `_hirr` constructor\n    argument), but the convention applies when irreducibility is\n    *part of a type's identity*. For `NumberField.Inv`'s case,\n    irreducibility is an *ambient assumption used by an operation*\n    on an existing type, so a class is appropriate. -/\nclass Irreducible (f : ZPoly) : Prop where\n  not_zero  : f ≠ 0\n  not_unit  : ¬ Hex.ZPoly.IsUnit f       -- IsUnit defined in hex-poly-z\n  no_factors : ∀ a b : ZPoly, f = a * b →\n                Hex.ZPoly.IsUnit a ∨ Hex.ZPoly.IsUnit b\n\n/-- Computational checker for irreducibility. -/\ndef isIrreducible (f : ZPoly) : Bool :=\n  if f = 0 then false\n  else if f.natDegree = 0 then\n    -- Constant case: f = C k. Irreducible iff |k| > 1 and |k| is prime.\n    let k := (f.coeff 0).natAbs\n    1 < k && k.Prime\n  else\n    -- Polynomial case: irreducible iff `factor f` returns a\n    -- `Factorization` whose scalar is a unit (±1) and whose\n    -- factors array has exactly one entry with multiplicity 1.\n    let φ := factor f\n    decide (φ.scalar.natAbs = 1) &&\n    φ.factors.size == 1 &&\n    decide ((φ.factors.get! 0).snd = 1)\n\nend Hex.ZPoly\n```\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:95:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_49","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"ls -1 HexBerlekampZassenhaus HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib 2>/dev/null; sed -n '1,120p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus.lean 2>/dev/null; sed -n '1,80p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean 2>/dev/null\"","aggregated_output":"HexBerlekampZassenhaus:\nBasic.lean\nBench.lean\nConformance.lean\nCrossCheck.lean\nEmitFixtures.lean\nSmallModSingleton.lean\n\nHexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib:\nBHKSBound.lean\nBHKSIndependent.lean\nBadVector.lean\nBadVectorAuxiliary.lean\nBasic.lean\nCLDColumnBound.lean\nColumnSignature.lean\nFactorSoundness.lean\nFiniteSumBounds.lean\nHotPathDiscriminant.lean\nIntReductionMod.lean\nLattice.lean\nPartitionRefinement.lean\nRecovery.lean\nResultant.lean\nSignatureClasses.lean\nTerminationBound.lean\nUFDPartition.lean\nimport HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Basic\nimport HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Conformance\nimport HexBerlekampZassenhaus.CrossCheck\nimport HexBerlekampZassenhaus.SmallModSingleton\n\n/-!\nThe `HexBerlekampZassenhaus` library exposes the executable integer\nBerlekamp-Zassenhaus factorization pipeline: normalization, good-prime\nselection, Hensel-lift packaging, LLL-based recombination, bounded/default\nfactor entry points, and the integer irreducibility certificate checker.\n\nThe library root also imports `HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Conformance` for\nPhase 3 core checks and `HexBerlekampZassenhaus.CrossCheck`, the\nSPEC-sanctioned LLL-vs-exhaustive recombination cross-check.\n-/\nimport HexArith.Nat.Prime\nimport HexBerlekamp.Factor\nimport HexBerlekamp.Irreducibility\nimport HexHensel.Multifactor\nimport HexHensel.QuadraticMultifactor\nimport HexLLL.Basic\n\n/-!\nExecutable data records for the Berlekamp-Zassenhaus factorization pipeline.\n\nThis module contains the shared records passed between prime selection,\nHensel lifting, and LLL-based integer recombination in the `ZPoly`\nfactorization pipeline.\n-/\nnamespace Hex\n\nnamespace ZPoly\n\n/-- The integer polynomial `X`. -/\ndef X : ZPoly :=\n  DensePoly.monomial 1 1\n\nprivate def splitInitialZeros : List Int → Nat × List Int\n  | [] => (0, [])\n  | coeff :: coeffs =>\n      if coeff = 0 then\n        let rest := splitInitialZeros coeffs\n        (rest.1 + 1, rest.2)\n      else\n        (0, coeff :: coeffs)\n\n/-- Data from extracting the largest visible power of `X` from a dense integer polynomial. -/\nstructure XPowerData where\n  power : Nat\n  core : ZPoly\n\n/--\nRemove the initial zero-coefficient run from a dense integer polynomial.\n\nDense coefficients are stored in ascending degree order, so the initial zero\nrun is exactly the executable power of `X` dividing the polynomial.\n-/\ndef extractXPower (f : ZPoly) : XPowerData :=\n  let split := splitInitialZeros f.toArray.toList\n  { power := split.1, core := DensePoly.ofCoeffs split.2.toArray }\n\n/-- The integer leading coefficient reduced to the candidate prime field. -/\ndef leadingCoeffModP (f : ZPoly) (p : Nat) [ZMod64.Bounds p] : ZMod64 p :=\n  ZMod64.ofNat p (intModNat (DensePoly.leadingCoeff f) p)\n\nend ZPoly\n\n/-- The candidate prime does not divide the integer leading coefficient. -/\ndef leadingCoeffAdmissible (f : ZPoly) (p : Nat) [ZMod64.Bounds p] : Prop :=\n  ZPoly.leadingCoeffModP f p ≠ 0\n\n/--\nExecutable test that a field-polynomial gcd is a unit.\n\n`DensePoly.gcd` is the raw Euclidean representative, so over a field it may be\nany nonzero constant associate of `1`.  In normalized dense representation,\nnonzero constants are exactly the polynomials with one stored coefficient.\n-/\ndef gcdIsUnit {R : Type u} [Zero R] [DecidableEq R]\n    (g : DensePoly R) : Bool :=\n  g.size == 1\n\n/-- The modular image is square-free according to the executable gcd-unit criterion. -/\ndef squareFreeModP (f : ZPoly) (p : Nat) [ZMod64.Bounds p] : Prop :=\n  let fModP := ZPoly.modP p f\n  gcdIsUnit (DensePoly.gcd fModP (DensePoly.derivative fModP)) = true\n\n/--\nExecutable good-prime predicate for the Berlekamp-Zassenhaus pipeline.\n\nIt checks that the modulus is at least `3`, that the integer leading coefficient\nsurvives reduction modulo `p`, and that the modular image is square-free.\n-/\ndef isGoodPrime (f : ZPoly) (p : Nat) [ZMod64.Bounds p] : Bool :=\n  let fModP := ZPoly.modP p f\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:98:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_50","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"gh issue view 2564 --json number,title,labels,assignees,state,body --jq '{number,title,state,labels:[.labels[].name],assignees:[.assignees[].login]}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"assignees\":[],\"labels\":[\"blocked\",\"directive\"],\"number\":2564,\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"title\":\"HO-1: BZ recombination implements wrong lattice — rollback and rewrite to van Hoeij CLD\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./sessions/10302747-02b0-470f-99eb-f2c018c87a42.stdout:103:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_53","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' SPEC/Libraries/hex-conway.md\"","aggregated_output":"# hex-conway (Conway polynomial database, depends on hex-berlekamp)\n\nConway polynomials are canonical irreducible polynomials `C(p, n)` for\neach prime `p` and degree `n`, satisfying compatibility conditions\nacross degree divisors: if `m | n`, then the image of a root of\n`C(p, n)` under the norm map `GF(p^n) → GF(p^m)` is a root of\n`C(p, m)`. This ensures that embeddings `GF(p^m) ↪ GF(p^n)` are\ncoherent.\n\nThis library has three distinct service tiers with very different\nperformance expectations. The implementation and API must keep these\ntiers separate rather than presenting them as one undifferentiated\n\"compute a Conway polynomial\" operation.\n\n## Tier 1: imported database entries with irreducibility proofs\n\nFor many commonly used `(p, n)` pairs, import a known Conway polynomial\nfrom Frank Lübeck's tables (or another explicit public source with the\nsame conventions). For these entries, the minimum contract is:\n\n- store the polynomial coefficients\n- prove `Irreducible (conwayPoly p n)` in Lean\n\nThis is the default path for finite field construction. Empirically,\nchecking irreducibility of a *given* polynomial over `F_p` is cheap\ncompared to either integer polynomial factorization or searching for new\nConway polynomials. Therefore this tier should be treated as the\nbaseline supported mode, not as a temporary fallback.\n\nThe intended engineering model is:\n\n- the committed Lübeck slice is generated into a definition such as\n  `luebeckConwayPolynomial? : (p n : Nat) → Option (FpPoly p)`\n- the generated code is ordinary Lean code checked by the kernel\n- support may change over time by regenerating this definition from a\n  chosen finite manifest or bound policy\n- regeneration should be driven by a command-level metaprogram rather\n  than by manually editing the generated definition\n\nThe intended metaprogram interface is a command named\n`rebuild_luebeckConwayPolynomial?`. It should take a scope specification\nfor the desired Lübeck slice, consume the immediately following command,\ncheck that this following command is a definition of\n`luebeckConwayPolynomial?`, and emit a `Try this:` replacement for that\ndefinition.\n\nThe emitted replacement should contain:\n\n- a new generated definition of `luebeckConwayPolynomial?` for the\n  requested scope\n- the rebuilding command itself, but commented out, immediately above\n  the generated definition so the file remains self-rebuilding\n\nThe point of this interface is that the committed table remains ordinary\nLean code, while regeneration is still one command away and leaves a\nclear audit trail in the source file.\n\nThe size of the committed Lübeck slice is determined by proof-checking\nbudget, not by mathematical coverage alone. The project should include\nas much of the Lübeck table as possible subject to the requirement that\nthe generated Tier 1 correctness theorems (for example,\n`luebeckConwayPolynomial?_irreducible`) still check in only a few\nminutes of runtime on the benchmark machine.\n\n## Tier 2: imported database entries with full Conway verification\n\nFor imported entries, a stronger contract is to verify that the imported\npolynomial is not merely irreducible but actually satisfies the Conway\nconditions relative to already-imported divisor-degree entries:\n\n- irreducible\n- primitive\n- compatible with `C(p, m)` for each proper divisor `m | n`\n\nThis tier certifies the imported table itself. It is still much cheaper\nthan searching for new Conway polynomials from scratch, so the spec\nshould aim to cover all committed table entries at this level whenever\npractical.\n\nThe same proof-budget rule applies here as the table grows: the\ncommitted Tier 2 verification story should remain within a \"few\nminutes\" regime for the generated correctness theorems on the benchmark\nmachine. If necessary, Tier 2 may temporarily cover a smaller subset of\nthe committed Tier 1 table while the stronger checker is optimized.\n\n## Tier 3: on-demand Conway search\n\nFor `(p, n)` pairs not covered by the committed table, one may search\nfor the lexicographically smallest monic irreducible polynomial of\ndegree `n` over `F_p` satisfying the Conway compatibility conditions\nwith all `C(p, m)` for `m | n`.\n\nThis is a separate feature, not the default field-construction path.\nThe performance profile is much worse and much less predictable than\nTier 1 or Tier 2. In particular, \"verify an imported Conway polynomial\"\nand \"find a new Conway polynomial\" must not be conflated in planning,\nbenchmarking, or user-facing expectations.\n\n**Sources of Conway polynomials:**\n\n1. **Hardcoded database** — commonly used `(p, n)` pairs, sourced from\n   Frank Lübeck's tables. The required baseline is Tier 1. The intended\n   target is Tier 2 for all committed entries.\n\n2. **On-demand computation** — for `(p, n)` pairs not in the database,\n   search for the lexicographically smallest monic irreducible polynomial\n   of degree `n` over `F_p` satisfying the compatibility condition with\n   all `C(p, m)` for `m | n`. This uses hex-berlekamp for irreducibility\n   testing. The result is deterministic (the definition of Conway\n   polynomial specifies \"lexicographically smallest\").\n\n**API:**\n```lean\ndef conwayPoly (p n : Nat) : FpPoly p\n\ntheorem conwayPoly_nonconstant (p n : Nat) : 0 < (conwayPoly p n).degree\ntheorem conwayPoly_irreducible (p n : Nat) : Irreducible (conwayPoly p n)\ntheorem conwayPoly_compat (p m n : Nat) (h : m ∣ n) : ...\n```\n\nThe API should expose the tiers explicitly rather than hiding them all\nbehind one partial-performance promise. Concretely:\n\n- `conwayPoly` should be total only for committed table entries\n- a separate API may request Tier 3 search explicitly\n- callers that only need a verified irreducible modulus for `GF(p^n)`\n  should be able to use Tier 1 entries without paying for Conway search\n\nIn particular, this library must not promise that every `(p, n)` is\nhandled quickly. The committed database and the search functionality are\ndifferent products.\n\nThe intended proof style for imported-table correctness is checker-based\nbut must not use `native_decide`. Project-wide, `native_decide` is\nbanned. The `hex-conway` table proofs should instead use explicit\nverified checkers/tactics whose runtime is benchmarked and whose scaling\ndetermines how much of the table is committed.\n\n**hex-gfq** then defines\n`GFq p n := FiniteField p (conwayPoly p n) (conwayPoly_nonconstant p n)\n(conwayPoly_irreducible p n)`. When a user asks for `GF(p^n)`, the\nConway polynomial is chosen automatically *when a committed entry is\navailable*. For degrees outside the committed table, a separate explicit\nsearch API may be used.\n\n## External comparators\n\nNo external comparator is required.\n\n**Justification:** `input-source-only` per\n`SPEC/benchmarking.md §\"Comparator naming\"`. The only published\nexternal reference for HexConway is Lübeck's Conway polynomial\ndatabase, and that database is the **input source** rather than an\nexecutable comparator: HexConway verifies that committed table\nentries are irreducible (Tier 1) and additionally satisfy the\nprimitivity / compatibility properties (Tier 2), and searches for\nmissing entries (Tier 3). The search direction has no\nalgorithmically distinct external implementation to race against —\nGAP exposes the same Lübeck data, not a separately-engineered\nsearch; and SageMath / FLINT either consume the same table or run\nad-hoc search code that is not packaged as a benchmarkable API.\nThe Phase-4 evidence is therefore the declared-complexity verdict\nof HexConway's own bench targets across the three tiers.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

they follow the same\nreports/phase4-5-checkpoint-48.md:128:  `HexPolyFp`, `HexGFqField`, `HexHensel`, `HexConway`, `HexGFq`,\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:1:# HexConway API Phase 6 Review\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:5:Reviewed `HexConway/Basic.lean`, `HexConway.lean`, and the conformance\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:6:expectations in `HexConway/Conformance.lean`.\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:14:The SPEC surface audited was `SPEC/Libraries/hex-conway.md`, especially\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:25:HexConway has the right core Tier 1 shape for downstream `HexGFq` use.\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:54:`HexConway Phase 6: characterise Tier 1 lookup support`\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:85:`HexConway Phase 6: regularise per-entry degree theorem names`\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:116:`HexConway Phase 6: polish lookup theorem docs and automation`\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:146:`HexConway/Basic.lean` imports `HexBerlekamp.RabinSoundness` and\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:148:`HexConway.lean` only re-exports `Basic` and `Conformance`.\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:158:covered by `reports/hex-conway-performance.md`.\nreports/hex-conway-api-phase6-review.md:172:HexConway should not be marked Phase 6 complete from this review alone. I’m narrowing this question to the separate acceptance-precision gap you described.
